# Plex Generate Previews

> GPU-accelerated video preview thumbnail (BIF) generation for Plex Media Server. Uses FFmpeg hardware decoding (CUDA, VAAPI, D3D11VA, VideoToolbox) and parallel workers to generate previews 5-10x faster than Plex's built-in method. Distributed as a Docker image with a web UI dashboard.

## Quick Start

Run with Docker (Intel/AMD GPU via VAAPI):

```
docker run -d \
  --name plex-generate-previews \
  --restart unless-stopped \
  -p 8080:8080 \
  --device /dev/dri:/dev/dri \
  -e PUID=1000 -e PGID=1000 \
  -v /path/to/media:/media:ro \
  -v /path/to/plex/config:/plex:rw \
  -v /path/to/app/config:/config:rw \
  stevezzau/plex_generate_vid_previews:latest
```

For NVIDIA, replace `--device` with `--gpus all` and add `-e NVIDIA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=all -e NVIDIA_DRIVER_CAPABILITIES=compute,video,utility`.

Open `http://YOUR_IP:8080`, get the auth token from `docker logs plex-generate-previews | grep "Token:"`, and complete the setup wizard.

## Features

- Multi-GPU support: NVIDIA (CUDA), AMD (VAAPI), Intel (QuickSync/VAAPI), Windows (D3D11VA), macOS (VideoToolbox)
- Parallel processing with configurable GPU and CPU worker threads
- GPU-to-CPU fallback workers for unsupported codecs
- Web dashboard for job management, scheduling, and settings
- Cron and interval-based scheduling
- Radarr/Sonarr/Tdarr webhook integration for automatic processing on media import
- Custom webhook endpoint for any external tool
- Library filtering to process specific Plex libraries
- Adjustable thumbnail quality (1-10) and frame interval (1-60s)
- Plex OAuth sign-in via setup wizard
- Path mapping support for Docker volume differences
- CLI mode for one-time batch processing
- REST API with token authentication
- WebSocket real-time progress updates
- Rate limiting and CORS configuration

## Volume Mounts

- `/media` (ro) — media files
- `/plex` (rw) — Plex application data where BIF files are stored
- `/config` (rw) — app settings, schedules, job history

## GPU Support

| GPU      | Platform | Acceleration     | Docker Flag              |
|----------|----------|------------------|--------------------------|
| NVIDIA   | Linux    | CUDA/NVENC       | `--gpus all`             |
| AMD      | Linux    | VAAPI            | `--device /dev/dri`      |
| Intel    | Linux    | QuickSync/VAAPI  | `--device /dev/dri`      |
| All      | Windows  | D3D11VA          | Native only              |
| Apple    | macOS    | VideoToolbox     | Native only              |

## Environment Variables

| Variable                 | Default   | Description                                      |
|--------------------------|-----------|--------------------------------------------------|
| `PLEX_URL`               | (wizard)  | Plex server URL                                  |
| `PLEX_TOKEN`             | (wizard)  | Plex authentication token                        |
| `PLEX_CONFIG_FOLDER`     | (wizard)  | Path to Plex config folder                       |
| `GPU_THREADS`            | 1         | GPU worker threads (0-32)                        |
| `CPU_THREADS`            | 1         | CPU worker threads (0-32)                        |
| `FALLBACK_CPU_THREADS`   | 0         | CPU fallback workers for GPU failures (0-32)     |
| `THUMBNAIL_QUALITY`      | 4         | Preview quality 1-10 (2=highest)                 |
| `PLEX_BIF_FRAME_INTERVAL`| 5         | Seconds between preview images (1-60)            |
| `PLEX_LIBRARIES`         | All       | Comma-separated library names or IDs             |
| `WEB_PORT`               | 8080      | Web server port                                  |
| `WEB_AUTH_TOKEN`         | Auto      | Fixed authentication token                       |
| `LOG_LEVEL`              | INFO      | DEBUG, INFO, WARNING, ERROR                      |
| `PUID`                   | 1000      | User ID (Unraid: 99)                             |
| `PGID`                   | 1000      | Group ID (Unraid: 100)                           |

## REST API

All endpoints require `X-Auth-Token` or `Authorization: Bearer` header except `/api/health` and `/api/setup/status`.

Key endpoints:
- `GET /api/health` — health check (no auth)
- `GET /api/setup/status` — setup completion status (no auth)
- `GET /api/settings` — current settings
- `POST /api/settings` — update settings (partial)
- `GET /api/jobs` — list jobs
- `POST /api/jobs` — create job (`{"library_id": "1", "library_name": "Movies"}`)
- `GET /api/jobs/{id}` — job details with worker status
- `POST /api/jobs/{id}/cancel` — cancel job
- `GET /api/schedules` — list schedules
- `POST /api/schedules` — create schedule (cron or interval)
- `POST /api/webhooks/radarr` — Radarr webhook receiver
- `POST /api/webhooks/sonarr` — Sonarr webhook receiver
- `POST /api/webhooks/custom` — custom webhook (`{"file_path": "..."}` or `{"file_paths": [...]}`)
- `GET /api/webhooks/history` — recent webhook events
- `GET /api/system/status` — system status (GPUs, workers, job counts)
- `GET /api/libraries` — Plex libraries
- `POST /api/processing/pause` — pause all processing
- `POST /api/processing/resume` — resume processing

## WebSocket

Real-time updates via Socket.IO on the `/jobs` namespace. Events: `job_progress`, `job_complete`, `job_error`, `worker_update`.
